title: Rawk
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Bantha}} {{Character |type=Jedi |image=[[File:Nat_Infobox.jpg]] |name=Nat Skywalker
(Bantha Rawk) |homeworld=[[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]][[Legacy (2006) 23|''Legacy'' (2006) 23]] |birth= |death=[[138 ABY]], [[Taivas]][[Legacy — War 4|''Legacy — War'' 4]] |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]][[Legacy (2006) 24|''Legacy'' (2006) 24]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=Blond {{C|[[gray]]ing}} |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Blue]] |skin=Light |cyber= |families=[[Skywalker family/Legends|Skywalker family]] |parents= |partners=[[Droo Rawk]]{{InsiderCite|101|Blaster}} |siblings=[[Kol Skywalker]] |children=*[[Ahnah Rawk]] {{C|adopted}} *[[Skeeto Rawk]] {{C|adopted}} *[[Micah (Cathar)|Micah]] {{C|adopted}} |affiliation=*[[New Jedi Order]] *[[Galactic Alliance Remnant]][[Legacy (2006) 47|''Legacy'' (2006) 47]] **[[Emergency evac fleet]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Family takes care of family.|"Bantha" Rawk, to his nephew, Cade Skywalker|}} '''Nat Skywalker''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] during the [[Sith–Imperial War]]. Born into a strong [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Skywalker family/Legends|family]], Skywalker was a descendant of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], the older brother of [[Jedi High Council (New Jedi Order)|Jedi Council]] member [[Kol Skywalker]], and uncle of Kol's son, [[Cade Skywalker|Cade]]. During his [[Time/Legends|time]] as a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], Skywalker [[Marriage/Legends|married]] the [[Kiffar/Legends|Kiffar]] [[Droo Rawk|Droo]] and adopted her daughter [[Ahnah Rawk|Ahnah]]; he also adopted a young boy named [[Skeeto Rawk|Skeeto]]. When the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]–[[Fel Empire|Imperial]] War began, Skywalker helped the [[New Jedi Order|Jedi Order]] find a suitable [[Planet/Legends|planet]], [[Taivas]], to build a [[Hidden Temple]]. After leaving the Order, Skywalker adopted the alias '''"Bantha" Rawk''', with his family taking the surname as well. In [[130 ABY]], after the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[One Sith|Sith]]-[[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Imperials]] [[Massacre at Ossus|massacred]] the Jedi on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], Rawk assumed that Cade had [[Death/Legends|perished]] during the attack. However, Rawk was eventually reunited with his nephew, now a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], and helped him build the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Mynock (Helot-class)|''Mynock'']]. During this time, he adopted the [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] [[Micah (Cathar)|Micah]] after rescuing him from [[Slavery/Legends|slavers]]. In [[137 ABY]], during the [[Second Imperial Civil War]], agents of the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] crime syndicate [[Skirmish on Iego|attacked]] Rawk's [[Rawk's Nest|home]] on [[Iego/Legends|Iego]]. Rawk and his family were assisted by Cade and the ''Mynock''{{'s}} crew in defending their home against the [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]]. They were eventually joined by the [[Imperial Knight]] [[Azlyn Rae]], an old friend of Cade's. The former Jedi Master revealed the Hidden Temple's location to Cade when his nephew expressed a desire to [[Assassin/Legends|assassinate]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Krayt. Rawk guided Cade's crew and Rae to Taivas and met with the reconvened Jedi Council as his nephew presented them with his plan to kill the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]]. The Council ultimately refused, and Rawk bid his nephew farewell as Cade left with his crew to assassinate Krayt, going against the Council's wishes. After Cade and his team had [[Battle on Had Abbadon|apparently killed]] Krayt, he met Rawk and his family on Droo's [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], [[Kiffex/Legends|Kiffex]], and asked for their assistance in [[Force healing/Legends|healing]] Rae, who had been grievously wounded during the mission. Rawk and Droo were forced to encase Rae in a life-preserving suit that Rawk had built for the [[Kiffu Guardians]]. When he discovered that Rae had not wanted them to heal her, Rawk gave Cade and his crew two days to repair the ''Mynock'' and leave Kiffex. Rawk later assisted the [[Core Fleet|Galactic Alliance Core Fleet]] in [[Evacuation of Dac|evacuating]] the planet [[Dac/Legends|Dac]], which had been [[Final Protocol|stricken]] by [[Viral spore]]s at the hands of the Sith. A few [[Standard month/Legends|months]] later, in [[138 ABY]], Nat Skywalker answered the call of the Jedi Council on Taivas and reactivated as a Jedi Master for the duration of the war against the reborn Darth Krayt and his One Sith. When the Sith [[Attack on the Hidden Temple|attacked]] the Hidden Temple, Skywalker fought alongside his nephew Cade, protecting the ancient Neti Jedi Master [[T'ra Saa/Legends|T'ra Saa]], who was engaged in [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] against the forces of the Sith. Skywalker ultimately saved Cade's life as he and Master Saa [[Beam of light|sacrificed themselves]] in an attempt to deal a critical blow to Darth Krayt's forces, allowing the evacuating Jedi of the Hidden Temple to successfully escape. ==Biography== ===Early life=== {{Quote|He understood the Force in ways I never could, natural-born leader—what folks look for in a Skywalker.|Rawk, speaking of his brother, Kol Skywalker|}} [[File:YoungNatSkywalker.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Nat Skywalker meets his nephew, Cade, for the first time.]] Nat Skywalker, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was born into the [[Skywalker family/Legends|Skywalker family]], inheriting the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force potential]] of that bloodline. Eventually, Skywalker grew into a powerful [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] in his own right. However, he felt he could never live up to his family name, especially in comparison to his younger brother, [[Kol Skywalker]], who was a natural leader and a member of the [[Jedi High Council (New Jedi Order)|Jedi Council]]. Skywalker was aware of his brother's secret [[Marriage/Legends|marriage]] to the [[Fel Empire|Imperial]] [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|spy]] [[Morrigan Corde]], although he never understood why until Kol brought his and Corde's son, [[Cade Skywalker|Cade]], to the [[Jedi Academy (Ossus)|Jedi Praxeum]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]. Skywalker raised Cade on Ossus, since Kol had to remain on the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], to tend to [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] affairs. Kol eventually brought Cade to Coruscant. During this [[Time/Legends|time]], Skywalker married a [[Kiffar/Legends|Kiffar]] named [[Droo Rawk|Droo]], and adopted her daughter [[Ahnah Rawk|Ahnah]] and a [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-sensitive boy named [[Skeeto Rawk|Skeeto]].[[Legacy (2006) 25|''Legacy'' (2006) 25]] By marrying Droo, whose first husband was a member of the [[Clan Vos|Vos clan]] of [[Kiffu Guardians]], Skywalker became a member of Clan Vos.[[Legacy (2006) 34|''Legacy'' (2006) 34]] When the [[Sith–Imperial War]] began in [[127 ABY]], the Jedi Council ordered the creation of a [[Hidden Temple|Hidden Jedi Temple]]. Skywalker [[Scout/Legends|scouted]] the [[Galactic Rim/Legends|Rim]] for a world suitable enough for the creation of such a temple, and came across the planet [[Taivas]], located in the [[Zhar system]]. Skywalker and his family helped the Jedi build the Hidden Temple. At some point during the war, Skywalker left the Order, revealing his reasons for doing so only to his wife. In order to hide the fact that they were Skywalkers, he and his family changed their surname to "Rawk," with Skywalker taking the alias "Bantha." In [[130 ABY]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[One Sith|Sith]]–[[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Imperial]] forces [[Massacre at Ossus|attacked]] the Ossus Jedi Praxeum, [[Death/Legends|killing]] Kol Skywalker and all of the Jedi on the planet and scattering the rest of the Order.[[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]] Rawk believed that Cade had died with his [[Parent/Legends|father]] on Ossus, but Cade had in fact been rescued by the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Rav]] and had joined the [[Feeorin/Legends|Feeorin's]] crew aboard the ''[[Crimson Axe]]''.[[Legacy (2006) 12|''Legacy'' (2006) 12]] At some point after the Massacre at Ossus, the Rawks rescued a young [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] named [[Micah (Cathar)|Micah]] from a group of [[Slavery/Legends|slavers]]. During the rescue, Micah lost his [[Limb/Legends|legs]], and Droo tried her best to [[Force healing/Legends|heal]] him. Rawk ultimately built and attached a pair of [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic legs]] to Micah and adopted the Cathar. Rawk eventually became a chief [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]] at the [[Selonian Shipyards]].[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]] He also maintained a side business creating and modifying weapons for [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] and other individuals who needed reliable weaponry.[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0½|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0½]] Eventually, Rawk and Cade, who was now a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunter, were reunited. Rawk helped his nephew and the [[Zeltron/Legends|Zeltron]] [[Deliah Blue]], a member of Cade's crew, build the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Mynock (Helot-class)|''Mynock'']]. Rawk also supplied his nephew with weaponry, building a modified double-barreled [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] carbine called the [[Rawk chopped special]]. At some point, Rawk attacked the ''Mynock'' as they were leaving Rawk's home, because Cade's friend, [[Jariah Syn]], had tried to seduce Ahnah. ===Visit from Cade Skywalker=== {{Quote|So, let me get this straight. You get mixed up with the Imperial Princess, run into your former Master, help the Princess escape some Sith, and end up on ''Bastion'' with Roan Fel. Oh, and half the galaxy knows you're a Skywalker ''and'' a Jedi. ''And'' you decide to invade the kriffing Sith Temple to rescue a Jedi you'd sold for the bounty, get caught, and nearly get turned Sith yourself. That pretty much it?"''
"''Pretty much."''
"''Just how many different kinds of ''idiot'' can you possibly…!|Rawk and Cade Skywalker|}} By [[137 ABY]], Rawk and his family had relocated to the planet [[Iego/Legends|Iego]] in a home nicknamed "[[Rawk's Nest]]." Rawk maintained a friendship with the Shipmaster [[Ure'monbarak]] on the planet [[Dac/Legends|Dac]]; the work that Ure'monbarak tasked Rawk with supported his family. Members of the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] crime syndicate attacked Rawk's Nest in an attempt to force Rawk to pay them in order for their protection services. The Rawk family was [[Skirmish on Iego|defending]] their home from the [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] when the crew of the ''Mynock'' arrived to help the Rawks. With their assistance, Rawk and his family managed to drive off the attackers. Four days later, during a [[Swimming pool|pool]] party, Rawk and Cade had a conversation in the Nest's workshop. Cade told his uncle of his recent exploits, which included [[Skirmish on Vendaxa|assisting]] Imperial [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Marasiah Fel]] in returning to her father, the [[Attack on Emperor Fel|deposed]] [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]]; and [[Duel in the Temple of the Sith|invading]] the [[Temple of the Sith|Sith Temple]] on Coruscant to rescue the Jedi [[Hosk Trey'lis]]. [[File:RawkFight.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Rawk overpowers Azlyn Rae.]] During Rawk and Cade's conversation, the bounty hunter mentioned that he had [[Sith training|trained]] under the Sith before [[Skirmish at the Temple of the Sith|escaping]] their temple. As Rawk began to berate his nephew, Cade mentioned that he had met his mother, Morrigan Corde, during his escape, and used Rawk's former name in addressing him. Rawk reminded his nephew that he had abandoned his former identity when he left the Jedi, and told Cade to leave Iego if he were to tell Rawk's children their father's reasons for leaving the Jedi. Rawk then gave his nephew information about Corde and Kol's relationship with her. Before they rejoined their family, Rawk apologized to Cade for never having told him about Corde, and asserted that family meant everything to him. As Rawk and Cade rejoined their family at the party, Skeeto told everyone to watch a [[Holovid/Legends|holovid]] that was airing. Rawk and his family watched in disgust as images were shown of Sith-Imperials on Dac [[Massacre of New Coral City|executing]] the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]], and also watched as the Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Krayt decreed that the surviving Mon Calamari in the galaxy would be [[Genocide on Dac|placed]] in work camps. The holovid showed an image of Cade and called him by his full name. Cade confirmed his identity as a Skywalker to his cousins, and Rawk began to scold his nephew. The two attacked each other, but quickly managed to calm down. Rawk ordered Skeeto to turn on the long-range scanners for Imperial activity, and told everyone not to panic. Later in the night, Cade was attacked near the ''Mynock'' by a masked figure. After he was temporarily incapacitated, Rawk sneaked up on the attacker and used the Force to [[Telekinesis/Legends|pull]] her blaster out of her hand. She gained the upper hand and held her ignited [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] near Rawk's chest. However, Rawk managed to overpower her, and she revealed herself to be [[Azlyn Rae]], a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] who had survived the attack on the Jedi Temple on Coruscant. They brought Rae inside, and she explained to the family how she had survived the massacre; she further stated that she had tracked Cade to determine whether or not to turn him in for the bounty placed on him. However, the long-range sensors picked up Imperial [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] entering the [[Iego system/Legends|system]] and heading toward Iego. Rawk instructed his nephew and his crew to hide. Searching for Cade, a scouting party led by Imperial [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Gurlok]] arrived at Rawk's Nest and interrogated Rae and the Rawks. Rae told Gurlok that she had assisted Rawk in defending his home against the Black Sun mercenaries, but the Imperial Captain doubted her and ordered his [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] to kill Rawk's children unless either he or Rae told him the truth. Rae used a [[Mind trick/Legends|mind trick]] to convince Gurlok to leave, and the Imperials went back to the Star Destroyer and left the [[Star system/Legends|system]]. Two [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] later, Rawk gave Cade another Rawk chopped special, and commended Rae for her actions. He recommended that Cade change his appearance, name, friends, and ship, and also advised against using the Force. Cade disagreed, and told Rawk of his intentions to [[Assassin/Legends|assassinate]] Krayt. When Cade mentioned that he could not find the scattered Jedi, Rawk revealed to his nephew that there was a hidden Jedi Temple. ===The Hidden Temple=== {{Quote|I keep turning it over and over in my mind. Cade Skywalker bothers me.''"
"''His ''solution'' is what bothers me. Assassination. My nephew's gotten darker. No debatin' ''that''. I don't know as his idea's ''wrong'', though. Back in the Clone Wars, Jedi got themselves roped into being ''generals''. More'n a few lives got took that way!''"
"''The Jedi got into that war and it nearly destroyed the Order. I myself gave into aggressive feelings. I ''know'' how ''dangerous'' they are no matter how ''justified'' they seem.''"
"''Different time, different war, Master K'Kruhk! Krayt's committing genocide and the Mon Cal are just screamin' for help! The Jedi gotta act!|Jedi Master K'Kruhk and Rawk discuss Cade Skywalker's plan to kill Darth Krayt|[[Legacy (2006) 26|''Legacy'' (2006) 26]]}} [[File:RawkKKruhk-Leg26.jpg|thumb|right|Rawk and the Jedi Council discuss Cade Skywalker's plan to assassinate Darth Krayt.]] Before Rawk, Rae, Cade, and his crew left Iego to locate the Hidden Temple, Rawk instructed Droo to relocate the family to her [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Kiffex/Legends|Kiffex]]. During the trip to Taivas, Rawk recorded a message for Droo and voiced his concerns over his nephew and the crew, as well as a recollection of how the Hidden Temple had come to be. After landing at the Hidden Temple, Rawk, Rae, Cade, and the ''Mynock''{{'s}} crew discovered that the [[Imperial Knight]]s Marasiah Fel, [[Antares Draco]], and [[Ganner Krieg]] had followed them to the Temple. Cade and the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Shado Vao]] [[Skirmish at the Hidden Jedi Temple|attacked]] the Imperials, but stopped when Cade's former Master, [[Wolf Sazen]], ordered them all to put away their weapons. Cade realized that Rae had led the Imperials there via a [[Homing beacon/Legends|tracking device]] she had secretly planted aboard the ''Mynock''. When Fel explained that Rae was an Imperial Knight, Cade—believing that the Imperial delegation were [[Spy/Legends|spies]]—tried to attack them, but was restrained by Rawk. Tensions died down, and Rawk, Cade, and the Imperials were permitted to meet with the reconvened Jedi Council, led by Masters [[T'ra Saa/Legends|T'ra Saa]], [[K'Kruhk]], and [[Tili Qua]]. Cade presented the Council with his plan to assassinate Darth Krayt, and they indicated that they would meditate on the matter. At night, Rawk and the Council discussed his nephew's plan. Rawk felt that his nephew probably had the right idea to assassinate Krayt, even if he was slipping toward the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. However, the Council decided not to support Cade's plan. Rawk bid his nephew farewell as Cade, his crew, Shado Vao, Rae, Draco, and Krieg—who were ordered by Fel to accompany Cade on his mission—departed to carry out the plan regardless.[[Legacy (2006) 26|''Legacy'' (2006) 26]] Rawk, meanwhile, returned to his family on Kiffex. ===Desperate measures=== {{Quote|There's nothing more that I can do for her, Bantha. It's like she's fighting me—like she doesn't want to live. To save her, she'd have to be constantly bathed in bacta—maybe for the rest of her life. We have to let her go.''"
"''Made a promise to Cade, Droo. Boy's seen too many of those broke.|Droo and Rawk|[[Legacy (2006) 34|''Legacy'' (2006) 34]]}} [[File:Rawk_and_Droo.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Rawk and his wife Droo discuss Azlyn Rae's fate.]] Eventually, Rawk began to work on a suit of prototype [[Armor/Legends|armor]][[Legacy (2006) 35|''Legacy'' (2006) 35]] on behalf of the [[Kiffu/Legends|Kiffu]] Guardians. When the armor was near completion, he informed [[Sheyf]] [[Zharia Vos]], leader of the Kiffu Guardians, that the suit was almost finished. After Cade's crew [[Battle on Had Abbadon|seemingly killed]] Darth Krayt, they rushed to Kiffex so that Droo could try to heal Rae, who had been grievously injured during the battle.[[Legacy (2006) 31|''Legacy'' (2006) 31]] As Rawk finished conversing with Vos, Syn contacted him over his comm system and asked Rawk to transmit the [[Rawk compound|compound's]] coordinates to the ''Mynock''{{'s}} [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]]. After sending the coordinates, Rawk and Droo met Cade and his crew outside their home. Cade, who was using his [[Dark transfer|healing ability]] to keep Rae alive, gave Rae to Droo. Cade deceived his aunt into believing that Rae wished to live, when in fact the Imperial Knight did not wish to live. Rawk promised Cade to do whatever it took to save Rae. Later, when Cade attempted to assist in healing Rae, Rawk refused, citing his nephew's usage of the dark side in conjunction with his healing ability. Droo tried everything she could, but Rae—who had been placed in a [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] tank—was pushing her attempts away. Droo believed that they had to let her die, but Rawk disagreed, stating that Cade had seen too many promises broken in his life. Despite hesitance from Droo, the two placed Rae in the life-preserving suit that Rawk had developed for Sheyf Vos. After finding out that Cade had lied about Rae wanting to live, Droo confronted Cade and told him to leave and to perhaps never return to the Rawks. Rawk gave Cade and his crew a deadline of two days to make repairs to the ''Mynock'' and leave. Two days later, Cade and his crew departed Kiffex without saying goodbye to Rawk and his family. ===Evacuating Dac=== Eventually, the Sith Lord [[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|Darth Wyyrlok]], who was the new ruler of the Empire, ordered the execution of the [[Final Protocol]]. The Sith [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Vul Isen]] executed the Final Protocol by infecting Dac's [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] with [[Viral spore]]s that would eliminate all life on the planet within [[Standard week/Legends|seven days]]. Three days after the Final Protocol was carried out, the Galactic Alliance [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] known as [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] discovered that millions of beings had died, which prompted [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gar Stazi]] to initiate a [[Evacuation of Dac|massive evacuation]] of the planet. Rawk joined the [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]]'s [[Emergency evac fleet]], and [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] his [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] to the docks of the recently captured [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Heurkea Floating City|Heurkea]]. He pledged to come back as many times as needed. Rawk and the evacuation [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] managed to save approximately twenty percent of the planet's population. ===Rejoining the Order and death=== {{Quote|The life I've made with you and the kits is far more important to me than the Order -- but this war has to be fought by anyone who is able to fight it.|Rawk to Droo|[[Legacy — War 3|''Legacy — War'' 3]]}} [[File:DeathofNatSkywalker.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Master Nat Skywalker, moments before his death.]] In [[138 ABY]], sometime after the evacuation of Dac and Darth Krayt's return to power, Rawk returned to the Jedi Temple on Taivas. Answering the call of the Jedi Council, he also reactivated as a Jedi for the duration of the war against the Sith, as he felt that the war had to be fought by all who could. He shared a holo conversation with his wife, who had also reactivated as a Kiffu Guardian after being summoned by Sheyf Vos, saying his goodbyes and professing his love for her. Later Cade and the ''Mynock'' crew arrived at the Temple to warn that the Sith had become aware of the Temple's existence. At first it was suggested that the Jedi flee while they could, however Cade then proposed a plan to lure the Sith into a trap which the Jedi, Roan Fel, and Gar Stazi agreed to. However, after the meeting, Rawk and Shado Vao confronted Cade about his plan as they did not like the idea of using younglings as bait; but Cade only reminded them that if they let Taivas become another Ossus then Darth Krayt would kill them to the last, younglings included. As predicted, the Sith Imperial forces, Sith Lords as well as the Imperial Navy and Army, assaulted the Hidden Temple. Inside the Temple's premises Rawk fought the marauding Sith alongside his nephew, finally patching their relationship up. Rawk sent a signal to the Galactic Alliance Remnant, and Roan Fel's fleet to make the jump to Taivas to trap the Sith Imperial fleet against the planet. However none realized that they were falling right into Darth Krayt's own trap. When it seemed that victory was assured, Darth Nihl unleashed a legion of [[Darth Krayt's Sith trooper]]s as well as the Sith Emperor's new [[Dragon ship|capital ships]] and [[Annihilator-class starfighter|starfighters]], all of which cut through their own ranks to complete their mission to destroy the Jedi and the allies. Although the Jedi suffered heavy casualties, uncle and nephew still knew they could deal a grievous blow to the Sith, and held out to defend Master T'ra Saa, who was immersed in [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] and had taken root deep into the planet. When it seemed that they both would die from a crashing Sith ship, Rawk pushed his nephew out of the area and into the water to save him, just as the ship crashed and exploded. Together in the Force, Masters Skywalker and Saa focused the life energy of Taivas and released it skyward in a devastating [[beam of light]] which destroyed a large number of Sith Imperial ships. Like his brother Kol eight years before him, Nat Skywalker had sacrificed his life to save his fellow Jedi as well as his nephew Cade. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Gotta enjoy what we got while we got it—be around those who we care about and who care about us.|Rawk, to Cade Skywalker|}} [[File:Rawk-LECG.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Rawk holds a lightsaber in his workshop.]] The Human male Nat Skywalker felt that he could not live up to his family's name. He believed that his brother, Kol, was a natural leader and what people looked for in a Skywalker. When Skywalker met Cade's mother, Morrigan Corde, he deemed her to be secretive and not easy to get a sense of. After Kol brought Cade back to the Ossus Jedi Praxeum, Skywalker was willing to take care of Cade while his brother attended to Jedi business. Rawk often berated his nephew when he felt that he had done or was going to do something stupid. When he spoke with the Jedi Council about his nephew's plan to assassinate Darth Krayt, Rawk opined that it was not a bad idea, even if Cade was slipping further toward the dark side. Rawk also felt that to ignore Krayt's genocide of the Mon Calamari was wrong. When Cade brought the injured Rae to Rawk after his successful assassination of Krayt, Rawk promised to his nephew that he would do whatever it took to save Rae's life. He was determined not to fail his nephew, encasing Rae in a life-preserving suit. After finding out that Cade had lied to Droo about Rae wanting to be healed, Rawk told his nephew to leave Kiffex in two days' time. Rawk's family meant everything to him. He adopted Droo's daughter Ahnah and a boy named Skeeto. After he and his family saved the Cathar Micah from slavers, Rawk adopted the boy as one of their own. When Jariah Syn tried to seduce Ahnah, Rawk attacked Syn, going so far as to shoot at the ''Mynock'' as Syn, Cade, and Blue fled Rawk's home. He believed in using caution and implored Cade to exercise this; however, he was still willing to help his nephew out by taking him to the Jedi's Hidden Temple. Rawk was unafraid to speak his mind and was reputed for being boisterous, traits that his family shared. Rawk understood a variety of languages, including [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]], [[Bocce/Legends|Bocce]], and [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]]. He possessed a blaster carbine and a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] that had both been modified, and also owned a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]]. When he rejoined the Jedi in the final stages of the war against Darth Krayt's empire, Rawk adorned a set of [[brown]] [[Jedi apparel/Legends|Jedi robes]] and utilized a lightsaber with a [[Color/Legends|yellow]] [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|crystal]]. ==Powers and abilities== {{Quote|Youth and skill, old timer…"''
"''Ain't got nothing on ''my'' experience.|Azlyn Rae and Rawk|}} [[File:NatSkywalkerisback.jpg|thumb|250px|Master Skywalker]] Like most members of the Skywalker lineage, Rawk possessed a deep connection with the Force. He trained as a Jedi, and eventually rose to the rank of Jedi Master, although he later left the Order. Even after years of not using the Force, he managed to overpower Imperial Knight Azlyn Rae, despite the fact that he was unarmed while Rae had her lightsaber to his chest. Rawk was skilled with telekinetic [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]], although his ability to use mind tricks had diminished, since his wife Droo forbade him from using mind tricks on their children. Rawk was a gifted mechanic, as seen by his construction of his adopted son Micah's legs after he and his family rescued Micah from slavers. Rawk also helped build his nephew Cade Skywalker's ship, the ''Mynock''. He designed a life-preserving suit intended for use by the Kiffu Guardians; he and Droo eventually used the suit to save Azlyn Rae's life. He also constructed the Rawk chopped special, a modified double-barreled blaster carbine that was Cade Skywalker's weapon of choice. Rawk subsequently supplied his nephew with upgraded versions of the carbine. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|Bantha and his family are the closest thing to family for Cade. ''[…]'' I see the Rawks as another layer in Cade's life where he can go when things get too hot.|Jan Duursema|[[Star Wars Insider 101|''Star Wars Insider'' 101]]}} [[File:Rawk.jpg|thumb|left|150px|A sketch of Rawk]] "Bantha" Rawk was created by [[John Ostrander]], the writer of the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series. Rawk was first mentioned in [[2006]]'s [[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]], a resource comic that gave basic information on some of the characters, locations, and organizations of ''Legacy''. A sketch of Rawk was featured in the same issue, although he was not identified as being the same character. Rawk was first explicitly identified in the [[2008]] resource comic [[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0½|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0½]].[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0½|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0½]] According to an interview with John Ostrander and Jan Duursema, Rawk would make a difficult decision regarding his life and destiny in the upcoming miniseries [[Star Wars: Legacy — War|''Star Wars: Legacy—War'']], a follow-up series to the ''Star Wars: Legacy'' comic series.{{WebCite|url=http://splashpage.mtv.com/2010/08/05/star-wars-legacy-comics-star-wars-legacy-war|text=EXCLUSIVE: 'Star Wars: Legacy' Series To Continue In 'Star Wars: Legacy -
